name: Holiday Gingerbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, heat molasses, margarine, and brown sugar until the mixture boils, stirring occasionally to blend well.
2. Remove from heat; allow it to cool.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour, ginger, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda, and nutmeg. Blend in the egg into the molasses mixture.
4. With a spoon, stir the molasses mixture into the flour mixture until smooth.
5. Wrap dough in plastic wrap; chill at least 1 hour.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Divide dough in half. On a floured surface, roll dough to 1/8 to 1/4-inch thickness.
8. Cut with a floured 5 x 3-inch gingerbread cookie cutter (or knife your own design).
9. Carefully lift the cutouts onto lightly greased cookie sheets using a metal spatula.
10. Bake for 10 to 14 minutes, or until no indentation remains when touched.
11. Remove from sheets; cool on wire racks.
12. Decorate as desired with icing and candies. Store in a tightly covered container for up to 2 weeks.
